body:
## Problem

The /tools/compare hub and /tools/compare/<slug> individual drummer comparison pages have a schema gap not covered by any open proposal:

1. /tools/compare hub (line 673 of api/meta/[...path].js): SSR meta present, but NO JSON-LD schema at all (no CollectionPage, no FAQPage).
2. /tools/compare/<slug> pages (line 684): emits breadcrumbSchema only — no faqSchema. These are high purchase-intent 'compare drummer gear' pages.

Confirmed NOT covered by:
- #1513 (covers /tools/tier-list, /tools/name-generator, /kit-quiz, /guess-the-kit — not /tools/compare)
- #1559 (/tools hub at /tools root — not /tools/compare sub-hub)
- Any open proposal in the 50-proposal backlog

/tools/compare is at sitemap priority 0.95 — the highest-priority unschemed tool page.

## Fix

File: api/meta/[...path].js

1. /tools/compare hub handler (around line 673) — add articleSchema:

articleSchema: JSON.stringify({
  '@context': 'https://schema.org',
  '@type': 'CollectionPage',
  name: 'Metal Drummer Gear Comparison Tool',
  description: 'Compare any two metal drummers gear side-by-side: kits, cymbals, pedals, sticks.',
  url: BASE_URL + '/tools/compare',
  publisher: { '@type': 'Organization', name: 'MetalForge', url: BASE_URL },
}),
faqSchema: [
  { question: 'How can I compare two metal drummers gear?', answer: 'Use MetalForge Compare Tool at /tools/compare — select any two of 60+ metal drummers to see their kits, cymbals, pedals, and hardware side-by-side with full spec breakdowns.' },
  { question: 'Who has a bigger drum kit, Lars Ulrich or Joey Jordison?', answer: 'Compare Lars Ulrich vs Joey Jordison on MetalForge. Both use large format kits with double bass; see the complete side-by-side on /tools/compare/lars-ulrich-vs-joey-jordison.' },
  { question: 'Which metal drummer uses more cymbals?', answer: 'Mike Portnoy and Danny Carey are known for having the largest cymbal configurations among metal drummers. Compare any two drummers cymbal setups on MetalForge.' },
],

2. /tools/compare/<slug> handler (around line 684) — add faqSchema with dynamic content:

faqSchema: [
  { question: 'What drum kit does ' + d1.name + ' use?', answer: d1.name + ' uses ' + (d1.gear?.drums || 'a custom drum kit') + ' with ' + (d1.gear?.cymbals || 'cymbal setup') + '.' },
  { question: 'What drum kit does ' + d2.name + ' use?', answer: d2.name + ' uses ' + (d2.gear?.drums || 'a custom drum kit') + ' with ' + (d2.gear?.cymbals || 'cymbal setup') + '.' },
  { question: 'Which is better, ' + d1.name + ' or ' + d2.name + '?', answer: 'Both ' + d1.name + ' (' + d1.band + ') and ' + d2.name + ' (' + d2.band + ') are legendary metal drummers. Compare their complete gear setups on MetalForge.' },
],

## Pages affected
- /tools/compare hub (sitemap priority 0.95)
- All /tools/compare/<slug>-vs-<slug> comparison pages (dynamically generated, high purchase intent)

## Estimated uplift
AI Overview eligibility for 'compare metal drummer gear' and '[drummer A] vs [drummer B]' query type. The /tools/compare pages are distinct from /vs/ (which already have FAQPage and Article schema) — they're tool-based and high-intent.

## GSC query bucket served
'[drummer] vs [drummer] gear', 'compare metal drummer kits', 'who has better drums [A] or [B]'.

## Verify
1. curl https://metalforge.io/api/meta?path=/tools/compare shows CollectionPage + FAQPage in response
2. curl https://metalforge.io/api/meta?path=/tools/compare/lars-ulrich-vs-joey-jordison shows faqSchema in response
3. Google Rich Results Test on https://metalforge.io/tools/compare detects FAQPage schema
4. /tools/compare hub page in Schema.org validator shows no errors

## Done
CollectionPage + faqSchema added to /tools/compare hub handler AND faqSchema added to /tools/compare/<slug> dynamic handler in api/meta/[...path].js.
title: SEO: /tools/compare hub + /tools/compare/<slug> pages — add CollectionPage + FAQPage JSON-LD (sitemap priority 0.95)
note: Fix was already applied in commit 07a64c3 (fix: #1706 add CollectionPage + FAQPage JSON-LD to /tools/compare hub and slug pages). No code changes needed — logging agent run state only.
